description: Small inline label used for status, counts, and categories. Rendered as a plain <div> with no ARIA role; the text content is the entire accessible name. Variants (default, secondary, destructive, outline) change only the visual treatment.

focus.notes: Not focusable by default. The component class includes focus:ring styles in case it is rendered as a link / button via className reuse, but the base <div> has no tabindex.

announcements: Reads the badge's text content in flow.
screenReader.notes: When the badge's color carries meaning (e.g. destructive for errors), pair it with text that conveys the same meaning; screen readers do not announce color.

contrast.text: All four variants meet WCAG AA against their own backgrounds in the default theme
contrast.ui: Outline variant uses the default border; still meets AA as a non-text UI element

motion.reducedMotion: Only the standard color transition on hover; no motion-based animations

consumerResponsibilities: Keep badge text short; it's read in flow and not announced as a separate region.
Do not rely on color alone to convey status; include text (e.g. "Draft", "Failed").
If the badge is actionable (opens a filter, removes a tag), wrap it in a Button so it becomes a real interactive target.
